tables.title: DKA vs. HHS Comparison
rows: ≥200 mg/dL typically (can be <200 mg/dL — euglycemic DKA, ~10% of cases)
Present — beta-hydroxybutyrate ≥3.0 mmol/L or urine ketones ≥2+
Metabolic acidosis — pH <7.3 and/or bicarbonate <18 mEq/L
More classic in type 1 diabetes, but occurs in type 2 as well
More typical in older adults with type 2 diabetes
Kussmaul respirations, fruity breath, vomiting/abdominal pain
Profound dehydration, prominent altered mental status/obtundation
Supportive care, IV fluids per protocol, airway protection, rapid transport
Supportive care, IV fluids per protocol, airway protection, rapid transport
